purpose.summary: This COBOL program deletes authorization details and summary records from an IMS database. It takes checkpoints periodically and abends if any errors occur during the deletion process or checkpointing.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph deletes authorization detail records from the IMS database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the account ID if it is (lines 4-6). It then executes a DLI DLET command to delete the PAUTDTL1 segment from the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS using the PAUT-PCB-NUM (lines 8-11). After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful (line 13). If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments WS-NO-DTL-DELETED (line 14). Otherwise, it displays an error message including the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program (lines 16-18).
This paragraph simply exits the 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

This paragraph deletes authorization summary records from the IMS database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the account ID if it is (lines 29-31). It then executes a DLI DLET command to delete the PAUTSUM0 segment from the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY using the PAUT-PCB-NUM (lines 33-36). After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful (line 38). If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments WS-NO-SUMRY-DELETED (line 39). Otherwise, it displays an error message including the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program (lines 41-43).
This paragraph simply exits the 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

This paragraph takes a checkpoint using the DLI CHKP command. It uses WK-CHKPT-ID as the checkpoint ID (line 53). After the checkpoint operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the checkpoint was successful (line 56). If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments WS-NO-CHKP (line 57). If WS-NO-CHKP is greater than or equal to P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ, it resets WS-NO-CHKP to 0 and displays a checkpoint success message including WS-NO-SUMRY-READ and WS-CURR-APP-ID (lines 58-62). Otherwise, it displays an error message including the DIBSTAT, WS-NO-SUMRY-READ, and WS-CURR-APP-ID and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program (lines 64-67).
This paragraph simply exits the 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

This paragraph handles program termination due to an error. It displays a message indicating that the program is abending (line 78). It then sets the RETURN-CODE to 16 (line 80) and performs a GOBACK to terminate the program.
This paragraph simply exits the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to SPACES during authorization detail deletion
DIBSTAT not equal to SPACES during authorization summary deletion
DIBSTAT not equal to SPACES during checkpoint
